content.problemStatement:
Given an array of integers, determine if it can be divided into two subsets such that the sum of elements in both subsets is equal.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN, the size of array
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers




Output Format

For each test case, print "YES" if array can be partitioned into two equal sum subsets, "NO" otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "YES" if the array can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum, otherwise print "NO". Each answer should be on a separate line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: total sum = 22, target = 11, subset {1,5,5} gives 11. Test case 2: total sum = 9 (odd) so partition impossible.
Test case 1: total sum = 42, target = 21, no subset sums to 21. Test case 2: sum = 11 (odd). Test case 3: single element cannot be split.
Sum = 2, target = 1, each element forms a subset.

solution.approach: The total sum of the array must be even; otherwise partition is impossible. Let target = total/2. The problem reduces to checking if there exists a subset with sum equal to target. This is the classic 0/1 knapsack subset‑sum problem. We use a 1‑dimensional DP array dp[0..target] where dp[s] is true if a subset with sum s can be formed using the processed elements. Initialise dp[0]=true. For each element v, iterate s from target down to v and set dp[s] |= dp[s‑v]. After processing all elements, dp[target] tells the answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * S) per test case, where S is total sum/2 (worst‑case O(N * 50000) given constraints).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(S) auxiliary space for the DP array.
